code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper: normalise object by stripping surrounding quotes
    def norm_obj(o: str) -> str:
        return o.strip('"')

    # Template functions for specific predicates
    templates = {
        "cityServed": lambda s, o: f"{s} serves {o}",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda s, o: f"{s} lies at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} lies at an elevation of {o} feet above sea level",
        "runwayLength": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a runway length of {o} metres",
        "runwayLengthFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a runway length of {o} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s first runway is {o} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s first runway is {o} metres long",
        "runwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a runway surface of {o}",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"The first runway of {s} has a surface of {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"The second runway of {s} has a surface of {o}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"The third runway of {s} has a surface of {o}",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"The fourth runway of {s} has a surface of {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"The fifth runway of {s} has a surface of {o}",
        "runwayName": lambda s, o: f"The runway of {s} is named {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": lambda s, o: f"{s} is operated by {o}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the ICAO code {o}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the IATA code {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the location identifier {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"The capital of {s} is {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        "foundingYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded in {o}",
        "mayor": lambda s, o: f"The mayor of {s} is {o}",
        "leaderParty": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} belongs to {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} holds the title {o}",
        "chief": lambda s, o: f"The chief of {s} is {o}",
        "hubAirport": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses {o} as its hub airport",
        "regionServed": lambda s, o: f"{s} serves the region of {o}",
        "nativeName": lambda s, o: f"The native name of {s} is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The demonym for {s} is {o}",
    }

    phrases = []
    for t in triples:
        obj = norm_obj(t.object)
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            phrase = tmpl(t.subject, obj)
        else:
            # Generic fallback
            pred_words = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
            phrase = f"{t.subject} has {pred_words} {obj}"
        phrases.append(phrase)

    # Combine phrases into a fluent paragraph‑style sentence
    if not phrases:
        return ""
    if len(phrases) == 1:
        return phrases[0] + "."
    else:
        # Oxford comma style
        return ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1] + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
